### DOCUMENT 1/121: 01 - How to Use Takeaways (Powerful Text Game Trick to Deal with Flakes).txt
------------------------------------------------------------

VIDEO: How to Use Takeaways (Powerful Text Game Trick to Deal with Flakes)
CHANNEL: Playing With Fire
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NlZooazanio

================================================================================
PART 1: FULL SPOKEN TRANSCRIPT
================================================================================

0:00
What's up guys? In this video I'm gonna show you how to properly utilize one of the most powerful texting techniques known to mankind, and that's takeaways. I'm gonna show you how to correctly do a takeaway in order to get around flaking, get girls from being non-compliant to become compliant, and bring back dead numbers.

0:25
So takeaways can range in severity. Some are very, you know, mild and subtle, and some are very direct and in your face. And the most important thing is that the takeaway you use must be appropriate for the situation. So for example, if this is the first time that she's being somewhat slightly non-compliant, and you hit her with a really harsh takeaway, that's actually going to work against you. So it's very important for it to be contextual. And you'll get a better idea as I go through some examples. So we're gonna start with the more mild takeaways, right?

0:49
So here's just a chick, you know, we're sending voice memos back and forth. I'm trying to build some investment over text cause I think she lived far away. Uh, she says it's kinda weird doing voice memos haha. I say, well, I would prefer talking in real life. Right? And then I give it a few days and she doesn't respond. So I hit her with 'If you're into that sorta thing.' So that's a very mild takeaway, right? I'm not directly calling her out, you know, I'm not saying, uh, why didn't you text me back, like I guess you don't want to talk anymore or something like very in her face, right? So hitting her with a very mild takeaway, which is appropriate. And you can see this kinda gets the conversation going.

1:25
Here's another, you know, more mild takeaway. So here I'm trying to make plans with this girl and I think this is the first time she's being non-compliant. So I say, actually tomorrow just filled up for me, let's do Fri or Sat. Okeydoke. Right? Like, what the fuck am I supposed to do with that? She has to pick a night. I say, which one? And she doesn't respond. I give it a few days and I say, 'Don't be shy.' And then she follows back with, 'Wait sorry my friends coming to fix my car today.' I say, 'Ok. I leave on Wednesday, but how's tomorrow or Monday?' And then again, she doesn't respond. So the second time that she's being non-compliant, I hit her with a more harsh takeaway. I say, 'Look if you aren't into this, just lmk and I can stop trying.' And that text is very powerful when used correctly, but again, you shouldn't really use that text unless there's been an established pattern of her being, you know, non-responsive or flaky. Then she says, 'This not it I'm just busy' and this whole thing goes back and forth. But eventually me and this chick do wind up meeting up and smashing. And a large part of that was, you know, me constantly doing takeaways and bringing the conversation back from the dead.

2:21
Here's another one. So this girl actually wound up dating for a while. So, uh, I'm trying to make plans. This is a cold approach number. I'm trying to get her to come to me. I say, 'Oh I do. Come here this time and next time I'll come to you.' Doesn't respond. Again, you don't want to use a takeaway right away. You want to give it a few days. But, you know, a few days have passed, she doesn't respond. So I hit her with a mild takeaway. 'Don't be shy.' She says, 'It's not about being shy, I would just prefer if we meet somewhere in the middle.' So now I figured out, you know, what her objection is. I hit that with a voice memo, and from there I was able to turn it around and get her to come to a bar near me.

2:54
Here's another one. So this is, uh, we're getting into a little more of the moderate takeaways. So, uh, you know, she's giving me the corona objection. This was like right when this whole thing started. And so I say, 'Don't worry, I stocked up on purrel and have a strong immune system. You're in good sanitized hands.' So kind of throwing some humor into the conversation. She doesn't respond, right? So then I hit her with, 'If you're too nervous, I'd understand.' And here she responds. And then eventually I was able to turn it around and get her to come over.

3:21
Here's another one. So, you know, I'm trying to uh, you know, make plans. I say, 'Good. I can always share a few secrets during our date.' Again, no response. So then I hit her with, 'If you're too nervous, I'd understand.' She says, 'Maybe I am a little bit haha. What are u doing tonight?' So you can see takeaways can be really, really powerful because most guys, they're not doing takeaways. They're not pulling back. They're actually pushing further. So when they get any kind of resistance, they just push further and further, which actually pushes the girl away. But when you take it away, then the girl moves in and fills up the space.

3:50
Here's another one. So, uh, this is a more harsh takeaway. And this was a chick who I had met, we had a really good first date, great connection, I walked her to her car, you know, made out with her. But then once I try to, you know, pull her back and get her to come over, she wasn't having it. So, uh, you know, here I text her 'Come by after,' she's like, 'I would, but the whole corona thing.' I'm like, 'We already hung out, who cares.' So she doesn't respond. So I hit her with, 'If you're not interested just let me know.' 'It's not that Alex, I really liked our date, I have a lot going on that you don't know about.' I say, 'So tell me.' 'I will.' I say, 'I'm all ears.' She's like, 'When I get off work.' Then she does end up telling me. Uh, she has a boyfriend and, you know, some complicated situation and then eventually we wind up hanging out.

4:43
